dialogos.texto_en: Oh, you guys are over here.
Ah, Uesugi-kun?\nWhat are you doing here?
You were just talking about the Ferris Wheel earlier,\nso I thought I'd check it out.
You were listening?\nWe have to be careful not to let our guard down.
Weren't you guys supposed to go to the ice cream shop?
We have plenty of time.\nWe plan to enjoy the town's scenery on the way there.
(Since they didn't go straight to the\n ice cream shop, there's a high possibility\n these two are not Itsuki...right?)
(Alright, let's try something.)
Ah, come to think of it,\nwe rode the Ferris Wheel together before.
I'm sorry, I don't recall such a memory.
Are you perhaps mistaking me for someone else?
Oh, was that not you?
(No one recognized that,\n so it's likely neither of them are Yotsuba.)
(Hold on, wait.\n Even if one of them were Yotsuba, she's now\n acting as 'Itsuki,' so she wouldn't know...)
Is everything okay?
No, it's nothing.
(This is surprisingly tricky...)
Since there's a Ferris Wheel here,\nwhy don't we give it a try? Not only can we see \nthe scenery of the town but also the Okinawan ocean.
In that case, why don't we ride it at night, then?
At night? But then,\nit'll be too dark to see the view.
Normally, that would be the case.\nHowever, during this time, the entire \ncity is decorated with light displays.
Each area has a unique theme,\nso it should be romantic\nfrom the Ferris Wheel's perspective.
(...I feel like Nino would know \n this kind of information, right?)
There's that kind of event, huh?
When I think of Okinawa,\nI imagined seeing the clear blue skies, \nnot the night view.
I see. Light displays, huh? That does sound beautiful.
However, even the lights of everyday life \nshouldn't be disregarded.\nThey're proof of human existence.
If you think about it that way,\neven a regular night view can look beautiful.
...That's true. This is a tourist destination,\nbut there are also many people\nliving their daily lives here.
I see...
We have to be conscious of the fact\nthat we are guests in this place.
Yes, if we forget that,\nwe might unintentionally cause trouble.
What? You're suddenly serious?
...Are you making fun of me?
No, that's not what I meant.\nI was just a little surprised.
Come on...\nEven I think about such things.
When you come to a tourist spot,\nyou have to be careful not to go overboard.
It should be a good memory for us\nif we're accepted by the locals.
That's why we have to enjoy\nthe local specialties too, right?
That's the kind of thing you tend to forget.\nEven though it's just common sense.
You get strangely excited\nwhen it comes to traveling.
I-I don't want to hear that from you!
Heheh.\nLet's both behave properly on this trip.
When you come to a tourist spot,\nyou have to be careful not to go overboard.
It should be a good memory for us\nif we're accepted by the locals.
That's why we have to enjoy\nthe local specialties too, right?
It's important to have that kind of awareness.\nWe both want to have a pleasant exchange.
That's right.\nAfter all, we're just tourists.
We also need to have a humble attitude\nthat allows us to learn about the local area.
When you come to a tourist spot,\nyou have to be careful not to go overboard.
It should be a good memory for us\nif we're accepted by the locals.
That's why we have to enjoy\nthe local specialties too, right?
...Hm?\nHow does that work out?
Knowing food is knowing the history of the land.
Eating the same things as the locals\nis what truly makes you accepted.
(Is this Itsuki, and her obsession with food?)
(But the way it was said just now,\n seems a bit like Miku...)
When it comes to local food, there's an old-fashioned\nOkinawan snack called 'muchi,' you know?
There's even a 'muchi Day' where people eat it\nto ward off bad luck, and pray for good health.
It's kind of like a sweet rice cake.\nAnd apparently, there are flavors like \nbrown sugar and purple sweet potato.
Hmm...\nI definitely want to try that.
Yes!\nLet's see if we can find it for sale.
(This Itsuki is also talking about food.\n It seems like they're two peas in a pod...)
